summary.brief: Zaragoza v. Adam. Medical malpractice case involving surgery general.

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff was admitted to Mercy Medical Center Merced complaining of abdominal pain, and she was discharged the next day. She returned to the hospital three days later complaining of abdominal pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A hepatobiliary iminodiacetic acid scan indicated plaintiff had a bile leak.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Morse offered the following statements and opinions: (1) plaintiff was an appropriate candidate for the cholecystectomy performed by Dr. Adam; (2) plaintiff consented to the procedure after Dr. Adam explained the recognized risks of injury, including injury to the bowel or bile duct, with possible bile leak;
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Eric Morse

evidence_index.text: Dr. Morse concluded that the attempted ERCP by Dr. Uppal 'set off a cascade of ongoing medical complications, hospitalizations, additional surgeries, and other procedures,' and plaintiff’s resulting injuries and disabilities were caused by the bowel perforation from the ERCP, not the bile leak from Dr. Adam’s cholecystectomy.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Eric Morse

evidence_index.text: The judgment is reversed with directions to vacate the order granting summary judgment and to enter a new order denying summary judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: Dr. Morse failed to support his opinions with sufficient factual detail and reasoned explanation to show the absence of a triable issue of material fact.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff’s condition worsened, and she underwent additional surgeries to treat the bile leak and related complications, including bile peritonitis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Morse also stated in conclusory fashion that '[s]ubsequent medical records confirmed that the attempted ERCP procedure resulted in a retroperitoneal duodenal perforation,' but he identified no specific medical records among the more than 1,000 pages he reviewed to support his conclusion.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Eric Morse

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was admitted to Mercy Medical Center Merced complaining of abdominal pain and was discharged the next day. She returned three days later with the same complaint.

focused_exam.abdomen: Examination findings not specified in judgment

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Abdominal pain with suspected bile leak

reveal.result_summary: Unsuccessful; unable to cannulate the common bile duct
reveal.clinical_significance: Failed to resolve bile leak, led to further complications

key_events.event: Admitted to Mercy Medical Center Merced

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes diagnostic procedures to identify cause of abdominal pain

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with confirmed bile leak after cholecystectomy
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing the bile leak?

options.description: Perform a successful ERCP to place a stent
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard procedure to manage bile leaks
Led to further complications without resolving the issue

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Procedure note indicated inability to complete

expected_action_court.description: A successful ERCP should have been ensured
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence suggested successful ERCP is standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: The unsuccessful ERCP led to a cascade of complications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: A successful ERCP or alternative intervention should have been performed
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed bile peritonitis and underwent multiple surgeries

key_findings: Summary judgment for Dr. Adam was reversed due to insufficient expert explanation
No triable issue of fact was established against Dr. Adam

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to provide sufficient expert explanation
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: A more detailed expert opinion could have changed the outcome

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to Mercy Medical Center Merced with abdominal pain

court_findings.finding: Dr. Adam's summary judgment was reversed due to insufficient expert explanation
court_findings.legal_significance: No triable issue of fact was established against Dr. Adam
